reviews.title: Great Facilities, but a Serious Billing Error Ruined the Stay
reviews.positives: The hotel’s facilities and amenities were impressive: rooms were spotless and well-maintained, and the property offered all the comforts you’d expect from a high-end resort. The housekeeping team did an excellent job keeping everything clean and orderly, and the on-site dining options and pool facilities were solid. Overall, the environment itself was exactly what I hoped for in terms of comfort and convenience.
reviews.negatives: Unfortunately, my experience was severely overshadowed by a serious billing error. The hotel mistakenly overcharged my credit card by adding an extra digit to the amount turning what should have been a five-night stay into a five-figure charge. To make matters worse, this happened twice, which caused my credit card to exceed its limit. What’s most disappointing is that the hotel made little effort to proactively inform me about these mistakes. I only discovered the issue myself on the third day after receiving an alert from my credit card company. For a property of Conrad’s caliber, this level of financial oversight and lack of transparent communication is unacceptable.

reviews.title: No standardized service....
reviews.text:
Stayed by two separate bookings in two different rooms on 19/7 and 20/7 night. Same room type. One of the guest's birthday.

Room are not standard. 19/7's room there's no TV in the bathroom and no cushions on the long sofa. I wouldn't have known that if I didn't stay on the second night.

Human's private part's hair found in the bath tube. Very disgusting. 

Facial mirror (circle shape mirror in bathroom)'s light wouldn't work. 

Coffee machine wouldn't work on the second night. Told the staff to replace me with a new one as I'm in a hurry to go out and need a cup of coffee asap. Instead someone come in take a look, and then ask another staff to come take a look again, and spent quite a time to fix it.

On second night 20/7 booking was made by my partner with his agoda account. Frontdesk insisted we haven't pay at agoda and double-charged us. in the mid-night front desk called and said they saw our payment and will void the second payment with NO apology. Disappointed.

reviews.title: Fair 5-star hotel
reviews.text:
We've reserved a non-smoking room with 2 beds and arrived at the hotel to check in on time, however non-smoking room is not available. What I appreciate was they proposed an upgrade to suite for us, unfortunately it wasn't an option to me this time as I do need 2 beds, so I just took the smoking room as I was told that not many guests were smoking ones actually. However, when we open the room door, the smell was so strong, that's also what we heard from other unknown guests when we had breakfast. There was also a long queue to wait for a table for breakfast.

Rooms are spacious but the design makes the room looks dim. You can however take the hand-made candies and the bath toy away. Nice sleepers and the ACCA Kappa toiletries. Price are relatively attractive when compared with other 5-star hotels located in the same region. Just a 5-10 min walk to Venetian.

reviews.title: Nice room, extra perks
reviews.text:
Stay was during the weekend in Macau so the room rate was higher than normal for a double room. Breakfast was included but a little disconnected from the lobby of the hotel. Took a while to find. 
Room itself was clean and for a double, was grand looking. No ciggerette smell which is a common problem in these Macau hotels with Mainlanders which is a plus. 
Hotel check-in took a while, and the front desk person was clearly uneasey with questions as to why check-in was no ready when it was already past 3pm. No real comp was given for the late time of check-in, even when asked. 

Hotel pool is not too large, but since most of the ones that go to Macau shy away from the sun like vampires do, the area does tend to remain empty. For a better pool check out galaxy.

reviews.title: Not impressed
reviews.text:
The best about this hotel is its location and breakfast. Other than that, nothing is impressive and rather disappointing, given that Conrad is a 5-star hotel and I paid 3 grands.

- Service: checked in at 3:30 pm. Spent around 20 minutes as the receptionist said she mixed up our first and last names. We asked to have our luggage sent to our room. But the luggage was nowhere to be found till 10:30 pm with 2 phone calls asking its whereabouts. When it arrived, the two staff members kept apologising. But when they left, they nearly forgot the luggage on the corridor, not realising that they had to at least put it at our doorway. 
- Amenities: very basic which is similar to a 3-star hotel. To be fair, the silk-like disposable slippers are comfy. But other amenities are quite basic. No disposable shower cap, Cannot dry my hair at the bathroom as there is no compatible socket. No USB-C charing hub so remember to bring your own plug and adapter. The wireless charging hub didn’t work with my iphone 15 pro nor my husband’s iphone 16 pro.

- bed and pillow: comfortable

- breakfast: wide array of food. Good and attentive service 

Overall speaking, it might be the standard service quality in Macau but I expect more with 3 grands …

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text: I was disappointed with my stay at Conrad. I had read wonderful reviews on the fantastic service and was looking forward to being pampered for the 3 nights stay. Unfortunately too many things went wrong. The junior suite was basically a longer bigger room and not a separate bedroom from the living room. I had to call for additional toothbrushes and toiletries for 2 nights even though I had registered both my daughters during check-in. I was asked what newspaper i would prefer during my stay but not a single copy was delivered. I had to also request for additional complimentary drinking water since the standard 2 bottles provided was for 2 people in a room. My request was not acceded immediately despite me telling the operator that i have 4 people in the suite. Definitely not worth the money for the service i received. The only consolation was when i called to speak to the manager and he extended our check-out to 3pm.

reviews.title: An average stay
reviews.text:
a short shuttle ride from the taipa ferry / macau airport, but if you are going into the city by taxi it will cost you approximately mop$50 one way. the venetian is just across the street so shopping and dining is not a problem but mind you the venetian is extremely crowded especially in weekends.

the check in queue was quite long considering the number of check in staffs, however the check in process went smoothly with the friendly staff. the bed was comfy but the tv is a tad too small with the size of the room.

the room was very spacious but on the 13th floor, i couldn't really enjoy the pool view that i have booked unless i look down from the windows. got a faulty tv remote control and an empty shaving kit in the bathroom but problems solved promptly by the staffs.

bathroom is huge with separate bath and shower, but at a newly built hotel with this class, it is disappointing that it hasn't equipped with rain shower.

a huge downside is the breakfast buffet is shared with the holiday inn in the same complex, even though as a conrad customer you have the priority to get in, do prepare for a lengthy queue at busy hours. the breakfast itself was okay.

all in all it was an average stay, with the price tag i might want to try some other newly built 5-stars hotel in the future.

reviews.title: Apart from this moment, the hotel is of high standard and we would recommend it to others
reviews.positives: We stayed one night at Conrad Macao and overall we were very pleased. The rooms are excellent — spacious, modern, with extremely comfortable beds and a nice view over Cotai Strip. Location is perfect, with frequent free shuttles to Venetian, Parisian and Wynn Palace.
reviews.negatives:
The staff was polite and attentive. However, there seems to be an internal inconsistency in the coordination between the hotel’s different departments. I booked the room through Booking.com with breakfast included for the whole family and clearly selected the ages of my children (13 and 17 years old). When we arrived at the restaurant, we were told that free breakfast is only for children under 12. We had to show our booking confirmation, after which the staff kindly agreed to provide complimentary breakfast for the older kids.
I believe the hotel should resolve such discrepancies internally with their own reservation department beforehand, instead of putting guests in the position of having to prove their rights and listen to explanations on the spot

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel with brand new amenities
reviews.text:
The hotel is brand new (less than a month old). The lobby was enticing as it was a good respite from the heat outside. However, the staff need to be trained to a higher competency. This may be attributed to teething problems.

I took a long while to check in at reception as staff was unfamiliar with dealing with guests who have pre-paid their rooms and simply providing credit card to have an imprint. The procedure took almost 1/2 hour.

When ordering room service, the staff were unsure as to whether the order can be charged to the room account and I had to be put on hold for a while for them to sort things out before finally given the green light to proceed.

Lastly, as I was about to check out and settling all my miscellaneous charges, the reception staff took about 20 mins to process my credit card. This was because the credit card terminals were faulty and my credit card details have to be keyed in manually at another terminal before the charge sheet can be printed for me to sign.

Other than the above, the hotel was lovely and comfortable on the whole.

2/ Check-out time at 11:00AM. Quite early as compared to other hotels. Spoke to the concierge, and they mentioned that that is the standard at "their" hotel. Anyway, was able to move this to the usual 12:00NN.
3/ Bar located at the Sheraton pools, which play loud music at night. Not really disturbing, but could still be heard.

Great location

reviews.title: Brand New Hotel with Lovely Service Details
reviews.text: chose to stay at the brand new conrad for the weekend.  the room is surprisingly big like a suite with a separate living room and wardrobe.  the nicest surprise is the nespresso machine installed in the room with free coffee capsules, which the staff refilled quickly.  the guest is also given a nice bottle of handmade candy as a compliment.  this gesture is quite rare in macau hotels no matter the class. the hotel shares the bodhi spa with the neighbouring holiday inn.  had an exceptionally good quality massage there.  restaurant selections is limited, but the venetian is just across the street.  definitely a much more comfortable stay than the neighbouring hotels.

Booked 2 rooms and travelled with the entire family with young children. Plus points includes the 2 queen beds, which is good for families travelling together. One of our rooms got upgraded to a family suite which was huge, and thank you for the kind upgrade. 

Getting around in macau is easy, so location of the hotel is good as well. 

Only improvement is for the conrad lobby to be more obvious as it can be easily missed when walking on the main belt since it's shared by 3 hotels. signages can be more prominent.

Overall, will recommend this property for families as it's not as smoky as the rest of the casino hotels, and there's some kid-friendly activities like the Shrek show etc that can keep them busy.

reviews.title: one of our Macao weekend trip HIGHLIGHT
reviews.text: Glad I ended  up booking with Conrad Macao Contai instead of the other popular and cheaper options like Holiday Inn (its adjacent hotel); the Venetian Macao Resort (so crowded with tourist in its lobby and everywhere...scary)... Best thing about Conrad is that they set it up in a way that you still feel you are in a 5 star hotel and not in a market place (with swarming loud tourist crowd ..). The Conrad staffs were really friendly and helpful. We even got upgraded to a Premier Suite. Our suite is a one bedroom suite that comes with a TV room, a big living room with a small dining table. Room is clean, bed and pillows are nice and soft - very comfortable. We were a party of 4 adults and the room can easily and comfortably fit 10 adults easily ! Will definitely stay in Conrad if I am back in Macao

reviews.title: Review of a 4-night, 5-day family trip: pros and cons.
reviews.text:
Advantages/
- Very spacious compared to other hotels in the same price range.
- Can accommodate three guests (Studio City cannot).
- The bed size is very large, so it's comfortable for two women and one child to lie down together.
- The breakfast at Churchill's Table is of very high quality (it offers over double the variety of food compared to the Fairmont Ambassador in Korea, serves steak even in the morning, and is available leisurely from 7 AM to 1 PM).
- The Changing of the Guard show held in the hotel lobby is of high quality compared to other hotels (like a small musical).
- Connected to the Londoner and Parisian hotels, allowing guests to enjoy each hotel's unique charm.
- I have visited London, Paris, and Venice, and the experience is not just a mere imitation of major cities; it is very well executed. There are objects throughout that evoke each city's atmosphere, providing plenty to see.
- Even in winter, there is a jacuzzi operated at 38 degrees, allowing access to the pool.

Disadvantages/
- The Conrad guest rooms and common areas have somewhat old designs.
- The overall ambiance feels dark due to indirect lighting at 4000K.
- The hairdryer has a high wattage but overheats easily and has weak airflow (based on people with thick hair, in comparison to Dyson and JMW).
- There are not enough electrical outlets.
- There are three sockets, so travelers will need a separate electrical adapter.
- Direct ventilation is not possible due to a window with no railing.
- The water pressure for the toilet is weak.
- The refrigerator is small and has poor cooling capability.
- Cleaning starts at 3 PM and can continue past 7 PM (the late cleaning time can be inconvenient for enjoying the hotel stay).

reviews.title: Good, boring, luxurious hotel
reviews.text:
Perhaps I have a unique taste from most Asian people, as I am a Canadian Asian, but this is too luxuriously uncomfortable for me.

I travelled with my fiancée to have a weekend getaway, and we were very pleased with the staffs' helpfulness and the room quality, these are two things you can expect from Conrad hotels. However, Conrad hotel was built right between Sheraton and Holiday Inn, so no matter which side your room is facing, you’ll only see the windows of the opposite hotel. The hotel lobby and lounge is directly in between the malls, making it seem less exclusive as gamblers walk by all the time. This hotel is classy, but feels too commercial as it's linked to high-end shopping malls, and somehow they only had Asian (mostly Chinese) foods without a single western one or bars.

The location is quite good though, as it is right between the Venetian and City of Dreams, enabling us to enjoy all three areas. Thus, we ended up spending all our nights in City of Dreams, where the SOHO area is a lot more lively with music and good western foods; a lot better than Conrad's classy lounge which for some reason lacks music and atmosphere.

Granted, I am the type of person who enjoys Miami beach and chilled night life kind of relaxation. So, if you or your wife loves brand names, wear Prada and Gucci all the time (including your shoes), quiet high tea, and simply don’t like going out much, then this hotel is perfect for you.

reviews.title: 5 stars hotel
reviews.text: I booked the hotel for our 1st year anniversary. we took the free shuttle from Taipa ferry to the hotel (hilton, sands and conrad).the check in was very fast. Room is huge, very clean. We got upgraded to city view on 35th floor eventhough the weather was very foggy it was still very appreciated. the staff recommended us to go to taipa village to try portuguese restaurant: el patisqueira (very authentic)which was a gem in the middle of the village. we highly recommend to reserve it. the hotel nicely offered a cheesecake to celebrate our anniversary ;). Will defitively stay again. the hotel knows how to make sure you will spend a wonderful stay with them.

reviews.title: Effort
reviews.text:
It was great.  We checked in to a non-smoking room that has a strong smell of tobacco. I complained and ask for a another room.  For this, we were moved from a regular deluxe room to a junior suite.  It showed great effort on Conrad's part.  The concierge was helpful and it even had a print out of our restaurant reservations which I thought was neat.

The food needs more attention though.  Buffet can do better.  I dined at Wynn's Chinese restaurant and I found it of higher standard.
reviews.positives: Good Service, Attending to our needs and location.

reviews.title: New hotel at the heart of Cotai
reviews.text: My friends and I stayed over in Macau for a night. This is the second time I've stayed there, and I have stayed in just about every hotel there. The room was large, we got the suite so we had a place to eat and relax and drink during our stay. Very clean and new, and the staff there was very polite and helpful. Price is good for this level of hotel. I would recommend staying there.

reviews.title: Excellent! Excellent! Excellent!
reviews.text: Was really happy with this hotel. Staff was very friendly and helpful. They upgraded us to a double queen size bed with no extra charge as we have two young children. Room service was excellent. They change our towels and made our beds twice daily. Hotel is walking distance to major shopping centres which is very convenient if you want to do major shopping during the day.
reviews.positives: Clean , friendly staff, 6 star standard hotel with excellent service and quality

reviews.title: Excellent hotel that totally worth it!
reviews.text:
- Excellent Service: Hotel staffs are polite and serve customer at heart
- Spacious Room: Deluxe Suite is spacious with dining area, living room, bedroom and a huge bathroom with rain shower, a soaking tub and TV
- Lovely Turndown Service: the housekeeping staff leave a lovely Conrad bear on the pillow which surprised me a lot, and the clean up is done with care
- High quality supplies; Comfy bedding, bath clothes; High quality coffee, tea, bath product with a lovely toy duck for bath; Sweet welcoming hand-made candies

Room for improvement:
Free Wifi is only provided at lobby and need to pay if you want to have it at your room. I was disappointed for a grand hotel.
reviews.positives: Excellent Service, Spacious Room and Comfy bedding
